analysis_description: Round 3 tape calibration: DTE at CSV day open 8/7/6 for days 0/1/2 per round3description.txt; intraday dte_eff = dte_open - (timestamp//100)/10000; T = dte_eff/365; r=0. Inferred csv_day per timestep by minimizing IV variance across strikes (same as live trader). Fitted piecewise-linear knot IV time-series and VEV_5200 median bid-ask spread by day; IV medians by strike from bisection on mids.
used_to_decide: Confirmed timing convention matches plot_iv_smile_round3; offline knot means were distorted by wing IVs so live strategy uses per-timestep median IV bands around knots instead. VEV_5200 spread median 3 ticks across days.

analysis_description: Backtest attribution: v0 PnL concentrated on VEV_4000/VEV_4500 (deep OTM) while inner strikes rarely fired Frankfurt thresholds; v3 EMA-S reduced noise and moved activity to VEV_5000. Grid sweep on STRIKE_5000_CAP (v6–v12) monotonically improved total PnL with cap=1 best at -265 shells vs -59385 baseline.
used_to_decide: Kept PWL smile thesis; added EMA underlying, skip 4000/4500, merge levels, cap wing and 5000 strike size; best cap=1 (trader_v12).

analysis_description: Follow-up Greek thread: assessed incremental delta-hedging of net voucher exposure with VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT while preserving the same PWL strike smile/IV-scaling engine. Also tested inferred csv_day gating variants. Hedge factors swept at 0.35 and 0.60; both degraded total PnL due to extract hedge losses outweighing option improvement.
used_to_decide: Do not add extract delta hedge in this branch line (v15=-547, v16=-852 vs v12=-265). Keep simpler unhedged v12 as best parent; csv-day gate variants were neutral in this setup.

analysis_description: Tape: PWL smile fit quality by strike. For each day 0-2, subsampled rows: implied IV from mid (bisect), knot IVs via same median bands as live trader, piecewise-linear sigma(K) between 5000/5200/5400; report mean/median |IV_mkt - sigma_pwl(K)| and |mid - BS(S,K,T,sigma_pwl)| with median vega. Wings (4000/4500) show huge IV residuals; inner band 5000-5300 is tight (e.g. day 0 median |IV res| at 5200 ~ 2e-4). Suggests focusing execution on inner strikes; supports stricter IV-scalping gating to cut noisy triggers.
used_to_decide: Implemented v19 (trade only 5000-5300) — backtest same total as v12 (-265). v18 (stricter switch_mean/THR) improved to -246; v17 periodic csv_day refresh — no change vs v12 (-265).

analysis_description: IV vs extract (tape): consecutive timesteps, corr(d log VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT, d IV) for VEV_5200 with IV from mid bisection and T=t_years_effective(day,ts). All three historical days show moderate negative correlation (~-0.39 to -0.42), consistent with spot up / IV down in short window. Microstructure: parent v18; added min bid-ask spread on voucher (v22: spread>=4, v23:>=5) to avoid trading when wall_mid fair is less reliable; v23 is best PnL so far (-101 vs v18 -246). v20 (v18+inner only) matches v18 PnL (-246). v21 stricter IV gates worsened (-307).
used_to_decide: Prefer v23 as new best: keep v18 IV-scalp thresholds and add min spread=5; continue grid from v23 (smaller next step) rather than v21-style gate tightening.

analysis_description: Book/spread: run_vev_spread_quantiles.py on VEV_5000/5200/5300 — top-of-book spread (ask1-bid1) by day. VEV_5000 is very wide (median ~6 ticks, high frac_ge_5). VEV_5200 is mostly 2-3 ticks; VEV_5300 often 2 ticks (frac_ge_3 ~0.17 day 0). So a global MIN_SPREAD is a coarse filter: higher threshold mainly screens 5200/5300 sessions. Bracketing MIN_SPREAD on v23 base: 6 → total -155, 3 → -215; v23 (-101) remains best. v24 (6) still shows +26 day-0 on VEV_5000 but worse days 1-2; v25 (3) worse overall.
used_to_decide: Keep v23 as the lead line; do not increase MIN_SPREAD to 6 for all strikes without strike-specific rules. Optional next: per-strike spread floor (e.g. 5000>=5, 5200/5300>=2) — not implemented this turn.

analysis_description: Per-strike spread floor refinement on top of PWL smile + v18/v23 logic. Using spread quantiles (VEV_5000 wide, 5200/5300 tighter), tested strike-specific filters rather than one global MIN_SPREAD. Built three mixed-parent variants (v26 from v23, v27 from v18, v28 from v25).
used_to_decide: All three variants tied at -101 (same as v23). By tie-break/simplicity, keep v23 as lead; strike-specific floors did not add value in this backtest regime.

analysis_description: Greeks on tape: run_vev5000_vega_theta.py — for VEV_5000, implied IV from mid (bisect, r=0), T=t_years_effective(day,ts), Black-Scholes vega and theta (added bs_call_theta in _r3v_smile_core). Median vega falls ~111→62 across historical days 0→2; median theta (per year) around -500 to -660 (time decay nontrivial at ATM).
used_to_decide: From v23 parent, grid: slightly looser switch gate (v29) improved total to -95 vs v23 -101; stricter gate (v30) -135. Ablation v31 (skip 5000) had zero trades — other strikes never pass Frankfurt-style gates under this engine. New best: trader_v29.

analysis_description: Family13 pivot (realized_vol_regime): compared fixed rv-threshold classifier vs rolling z-score classifier on extract realized vol (window 120; z-window 400). Data-first comparison in family13_classifier_comparison.json: agreement ~0.62-0.68 across days; z-score classifier is less concentrated in 'low' regime and slightly more transition-heavy. Implemented fixed (v32), z-score (v33), and retuned z-score (v34), plus stronger fixed coupling (v35).
used_to_decide: All classifier variants tie at -101 total on days 0-2 and identical/similar fill profiles. No evidence that rolling z-score beats fixed under current execution wiring; keep simpler fixed-threshold classifier if family13 must be retained. Overall best in branch remains v29 (-95), so family13 did not improve benchmark robustness or PnL this turn.

analysis_description: Duplicate-check follow-up pivot within family13: switched to a distinct nearby variant (realized-vol classifier with hysteresis/persistence). Data-first comparison in family13_hysteresis_classifier_comparison.json contrasts fixed-hysteresis vs z-hysteresis regime paths (entry/exit bands + hold=40). Implemented v36/v37/v38 and stored full worse-fill logs + fill summaries.
used_to_decide: Even with hysteresis, fixed and z-score classifiers remain tied (-101) and close to previous family13 runs; z-retuned variant is worse (-109). Family13 remains below branch best v29 (-95).

analysis_description: Priority analysis on underlying->voucher response/spread behavior by strike (underlying_to_voucher_response.json): same-tick beta declines with strike (day0 approx 5000:0.65, 5200:0.43, 5300:0.27), negligible lag beta, mild spread widening in mid strikes after extract shocks. Incorporated by strike-specific spread floors and beta-aware edge scaling in v39-v41.
used_to_decide: v39 (beta+spread) improved from v23 -101 to -96. v40 over-weighted beta and worsened to -111. v41 (spread floors only) tied branch best at -95 with simpler logic, so keep v41 as lead by tie-break rule.

analysis_description: Up/down beta asymmetry rollup (underlying_up_down_asymmetry_rollup.json): for VEV_5000-5500, |beta_up-beta_down| is tiny across days 0-2 (max abs ~0.02), so asymmetric quoting from separate up/down elasticities is not supported. Prior branch IV–extract correlation (neg) motivates mild directional THR_OPEN split using EMA of signed extract return: easier bid edge when spot rising, easier offer when falling. Implemented v42 (coef 0.12) and v43 (0.08); both match v41 at -95 total and 4184 trades.
used_to_decide: No PnL gain vs v41; keep v41 as simpler lead unless a future variant needs momentum asymmetry.

analysis_description: Substantive PWL smile fit on tape: run_tape_pwl_misfit.py (mid - BS with sigma from same three-knot PWL and knot IVs from 5000-5500 medians, T=t_years_effective, IV from bisection). tape_pwl_misfit_by_strike.json: VEV_5200 has near-zero median misfit all days; VEV_5400 shows large negative median misfit (-2.5 to -3.1 ticks) on every historical day, i.e. market mid structurally below the PWL-mapped model price at that strike. VEV_5100/5300/5500 misfit signs vary by day (wing/skew not captured by two interior segments). Delta medians in JSON support Greek thread (e.g. 5000~0.92-0.95 delta, 5500~0.07-0.08).
used_to_decide: Skip trading VEV_5400 in v44 (poor PWL map); v45 also skips 5500. Both tie v41 at -95 PnL and 4184 trades — 5400/5500 were not meaningful PnL levers in this backtest, so no change to lead; keep v41 for simplicity.

analysis_description: Day-conditioned misfit follow-up from tape_pwl_misfit_by_strike.json, focused on traded core VEV_5000: day0 median misfit is notably negative (~-0.30) while days1-2 are near zero. Hypothesis: day0 should use a stricter open threshold to avoid opens when model overvalues option mid. Implemented day map in inferred csv_day: THR_OPEN day0 > day1/day2, with small grid (v46 day0=0.50, v47 day0=0.52).
used_to_decide: Day-conditioned threshold worsened robustness: v46 = -109, v47 = -123 (vs v41=-95), with losses concentrated in day2 where stricter day0 policy did not help. Revert to simpler global THR_OPEN in v41.

analysis_description: Underlying shock vs voucher spread (tape): run_tape_extract_shock_spread.py compares L1 spread on timestamps where extract |log-ret| is in top decile vs baseline; for VEV_5000/5200/5300 median spread is unchanged (0 tick widen) on all days — book does not widen when extract moves sharply, so model edge is less protected by microstructure. Greek follow-up: added bs_gamma in _r3v_smile_core; v48/v49 scale THR_OPEN/THR_CLOSE up on raw extract mid jumps (|S-S_prev| threshold) with bump capped by gamma*GAMMA_SCALE to reflect convexity risk.
used_to_decide: v48 and v49 both reach -93 total (improved day1 vs v41 -51 vs -53; day0/2 unchanged). Tied at -93: prefer v48 (stricter shock threshold, fewer false shock states) as new lead over v41 (-95).

analysis_description: Read round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt: tight = both s5200 and s5300 L1 spread <=2; day0 shows higher mean 20-bar forward extract mid change when tight vs not (t-stat in summary) and P(both tight) ~0.10. inclineGod/ Sonic framing: use joint book as regime, not mid-only. Merged main added Round-3 day-3 tape; backtests now run days 0-3. Implemented v50/v51 on v48: multiply existing gamma*shock-scaled THR by 0.92 when joint tight else 1.04 (v51 stronger 0.88/1.08).
used_to_decide: v50 (mild 0.92/1.04) matches v48 on worse total (-93 over days 0-3); v51 is worse at -120. Record both match modes: worse and all are identical in these runs. New lead: trader_v50.py (PWL+shock+joint-tight as execution overlay); avoid v51. Still far from 200k/day objective — need extract/VEV-only redesign next.

analysis_description: Scope change: deprioritize legacy PWL line; reread round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt (Sonic: both 5200 and 5300 spread <=2; inclineGod: spread/spread, not just price). Implemented new traders v52/v53: joint L1 spread gate, extract mid momentum (EMA of tick d(wall_mid)), VELVET + 5200/5300 only (no HYDROGEL). v53 = larger/looser grid.
used_to_decide: Backtest: naive momentum-on-tight is strongly negative (v52 ~ -18.2k total on days 0-3 worse, v53 much worse) — mid-forward edge from the paper does not survive bid/ask and our order style. Best among new line is v52 over v53. Next iteration should add passive maker logic or a different entry (still gated on joint <=2) rather than taker-chasing EMA. Nowhere near 200k/day floor.
